entry.title: SAP B1 for HANA dependencies
entry.content:
Hi,


We're trying to rebuild an old HANA B1 9.3 PL09 server but stuck because of lacking repository libgcrypt.so.11 for SLES 12 SP3. Tried going through difference versions of the SLES ISO but no luck.

Any help would be much appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: How to automatically link Production order to change records
entry.content:
Is there way to link the production order to change record automatically on creating it manually.

Also for EBOM, Shopfloor Routing auto linking possible in manual method?

entry.title: Re: 1:n product substitution in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

  1. To achieve the kind of product substitution you are describing, where Product A is always substituted by a combination of Products B, C, and D in equal measure regardless of stock levels, you will need to use SAP S/4HANA’s product substitution features with specific configuration.

Steps to Configure Product Substitution

  1. Create Substitution Groups:

    • Use the ‘Manage Substitution Groups’ app to create a substitution group Supply Chain - Using Groups in Product Substitutions that Groups B, C, and D can share.

  2. Define Substitution Controls:

    • Use the ‘Manage Substitution Controls’ app to assign the substitution group to a control instance.*

  3. Configure Product Substitution:

    • In the ‘Manage Substitutions’ app, specify Product A Supply Chain - Manage Substitutions and define an equal division substitution rule to distribute the demand among Products B, C, and D.
    • Set the substitution to not traverse further substitutions in the chain by using an exit indicator.*

  4. Set Conversion Factors:

    • Ensure you set the numerator and denominator for conversion Supply Chain - How to Create and Configure a Product Substitution such that one unit of Product A substitutes equally for one unit each of Products B, C, and D.*

  5. Activate and Test:

    • Activate your substitution settings and perform a test by creating a sales order to check if the substitution rules distribute the order quantity as desired.

This setup should allow you to specify a direct substitution that forces Product A to be equally replaced by a combination of Products B, C, and D without depending on stock levels, by ensuring that all substitutes are considered a single output rather than sequential alternatives.*

 

2. To substitute Product A with a combination of Products B, C, and D while allowing easy adjustments to the distribution ratio, follow these steps:

Steps to Configure Product Substitution

  1. Create Substitution Groups:

    • Use the ‘Manage Substitution Groups’ app to create a substitution group that includes Products B, C, and D.

  2. Define Substitution Controls:

    • Use the ‘Manage Substitution Controls’ app to assign the substitution group to a control instance.

  3. Configure Product Substitution:

    • In the ‘Manage Substitutions’ app, How to Create and Configure a Product Substitution specify Product A and create substitution rules for Products B, C, and D.
    • Define the ratios for the substitution. Initially, set it to 1/3 B, 1/3 C, and 1/3 D.

  4. Set Conversion Factors:

    • Use conversion factors to distribute demand proportionally. Example: If you want an equal split, use 1:1:1 for the numerator.

  5. Activate and Test:

    • Activate your substitution settings and perform tests by creating orders to verify distribution among B, C, and D.

Adjusting Distribution Ratios

  • When needed, adjust the distribution from 1/3 B, 1/3 C, and 1/3 D to 1/6 B, 1/6 C, and 2/3 D by changing the conversion factors.

    • Modify the numerator and denominator in conversion settings.
    • Update the substitution rules to reflect the new desired split.

  • This flexibility allows you to dynamically change the proportions without altering the underlying substitution framework. Substitutions

By following the above configuration, Product A can be systematically substituted by varying proportions of Products B, C, and D based on business needs and inventory levels.

 

Best regards,

 

Dequan Xu

entry.content:

I was facing the same issue and adding this property now works:

inboundservices.monitoring.enabled = true

entry.content:
You can find all the released API and CDS views of worker here.
API, CDS

entry.title: Re: How to create outbound delivery during BD3/BDK (Third...
entry.content:
Hello,

In the standard BD3 or BDK process, Sales - Third-Party Order Processing typically, the outbound delivery is not created as goods are shipped directly from the third-party vendor to the customer. However, if there’s a need to create delivery documents at your company, you can consider adding a manual procedure to generate delivery notes.

Steps to Consider:

  1. Manual Creation of Delivery Documents: Delivery Management - Creating Outbound Deliveries You can manually create delivery documents within the system using apps such as ‘Create Outbound Delivery - without Order Reference.’ This allows you to document shipments even when no outbound delivery is automatically created.

  2. Use Business Process Scheduling: Adjustments in Business Process Scheduling Supply Chain - Use BPS with Outbound Delivery (BPS) can facilitate flexible scheduling and adaption of document types to create delivery documentation even if automatic deliveries are not part of the standard process.

  3. Customization and Configuration: Within your SAP configuration environment, you might explore options such as defining custom delivery types or using APIs to create outbound deliveries based on internal needs rather than external triggers.

  4. Integration with Other Processes: Consider integrating the documentation needs with related processes, such as billing or goods receipt, to maintain a streamlined workflow despite deviations from standard processes.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Output Items per Application Object Type
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides an application called ‘Manage Output Items’, which allows users to manage output documents like invoices, memos, and sales orders. The app does not have built-in functionality to restrict application types for specific business roles, which can lead to situations where users might accidentally handle documents irrelevant to their role.

Suggested Solutions

  1. Role-Specific Filters and Views:

    • While SAP allows users to set filters within the app, creating role-specific views might help alleviate SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – How to restrict filter values in SAP Fiori apps some of the issues. Users can configure views that preset certain filters to display only relevant documents.
  1.  

  2. Authorization Control:

    • Consider using explicit authorizations to restrict access based on specific business roles. You can do this by identifying the AuthObjects related to the outputs and setting restrictions.

  3. Custom Roles:

    • Develop custom roles with specific restrictions Tips & tricks to build a custom purchaser role with authorizationthat can help control access. The process involves adjusting catalog assignments and maintaining restrictions for relevant output types.

  4. Functional Area Restriction:

    • Leverage newly introduced functional area restrictions Get Ready Now: The Functional Area Restriction Typ… - SAP Community to fine-tune access further. This is particularly useful for tailoring business roles to meet specific needs without changing system-wide behavior.

By combining these strategies, businesses can better manage output items and reduce the risk of inappropriate document handling.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Customizing Field Values – Part 3: How to Use Mathematical Formula with Database Fields
entry.content:
Hi @OferOz ,

That's a significant improvement in WC usage!

I have a question: In the example you provided, the BP balance does not have any amount formatting. Is it currently possible to format the UDV output to display values like 1,000.00?

Thank you in advance!

Best regards,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Whether other incoterms can also applied to 5MQ Sell...
entry.content:
Hello,

The SAP Help Portal explains that valuated stock in transit Sell from Stock with Valuated Stock in Transit is applicable in scenarios where the risk and control of goods are transferred later, such as with the FCA (Free Carrier) and DAP (Delivered at Place) incoterms. This process enables the continued valuation of stock during transit until control is transferred to the customer.

Using FOB Incoterm

The FOB (Free On Board) incoterm typically signifies that the risk and control of goods are transferred to the buyer once the goods are loaded onto the transport vessel at the seller’s location. In such cases, the goods are considered in control of the buyer, and SAP’s valuated stock in transit process may not conventionally apply, as this process is generally used when the risk transfer occurs after dispatch.

Adjusting External Control Date

Even if you use FOB and the risk transfers earlier, theoretically, you could still manage the external control date in the delivery note. However, since the risk is already transferred during shipment loading with FOB, the application of valuated stock in transit might not bring the intended benefits. The practical implementation should reflect the actual business and accounting requirements specific to your scenario.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: RAP Action update Entity even on fail
entry.content:
@AbapForEver 
How about commenting out the following line?

APPEND CORRESPONDING #( <reg> ) TO failed-zentity.

entry.title: I want retrive Data for Procuremnet
entry.content:
I want to WBS element using Join ESLL and EKKN. when I join EKKO,EKPO,EKKN,ESLL. For Material PO Data retrieve Correct but for the Service Line , If I have 3 different WBS element for Service PO and 6 entries in ESLL so for 1 WBS element 5 rows get repeated.So Total I have 18 records.

Join I used as follows.

EKKO-EKPO-     EBELN

EKPO-EKKN-      EBENP EBELN

EKPO-ESLL        PACKNO-PackNO-SubpackNo

entry.title: Re: Customizing Field Values – Part 3: How to Use Mathematical Formula with Database Fields
entry.content: Hi @Felipe_Lima , currently it's not possible

entry.title: Re: Navigate from custom tile to specific section in People Profile (including custom section)
entry.content:
Hello, 

cool trick! 

is it possible to create such a link and locale it under Actions to make it easy for employees to find some sections on his/her profile? 

i used the same link and it worked, but before the system scroll down to the needed section, the page is refreshed. I understand that it is because in the link we have the direction "people profile", but when i tried to delete it from the link,  it did not work and i saw the error "page is not found". 

What i expect: when i am already on my profile, after clicking on the button under "Actions" with the link to the section, the page just scrolled down to the needed section. Without reloading the whole page. 

any idea?

thanks,

a

entry.title: Re: Second ledger HGB
entry.content: Hey Batista, may I can ask for more details? Many thanks Sebastian

entry.title: Re: CR for VS dropped after 2027?
entry.content: Sorry if I wasn't clear... No you cannot mix versions on the same PC. And in 2026 there won't be 32bit runtime available. CR for VS runtime uses the same name spaces for x86 and x64. If you change the platform from AnyCPU - x86 -> x64 and look at the path the runtime is loading from you'll see the folder changes in VS 2022 to the CR refferences.

Thank you!

entry.title: EKPO showing different ADRNR for single PO, due to which it is printing differently. How does for sa
entry.content:
EKPO showing different ADRNR for single PO(Say 4 line PO), due to which it is printing differently.

How does for same PO different ADRNR got generated in EKPO table for few cases. For few cases it is blank and for few cases it is same ADRNR accross PO line items in EKPO.

And how ADRNR update can be restricted in EKPO, it should print same for all line items.

entry.title: How to increase the invoice approve number list in SAP FIORI?
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to increase the invoice number list in SAP Fiori but I cannot find out how to do that.

For example, if I have 50 vendor invoices to approve, in Fiori I`m able to see only the first 20.

I could see all the vendor list invoices that needs to be approved in SAP, but in Fiori I could see at most 20 of them.

How to increase the vendor invoice number list in Fiori?

Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Email Notifications
entry.content: @Abhinav1 - Yes we were able to, your exchange server should allow the sender to send email. Sender here is the cloudalm.

entry.title: Type-ahead with search help exit that calls external API?
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is it possible to enable type-ahead search on a field that uses a search help exit to get values using ABAP code rather than from a view in ECC 7.40 SP 32?

I would like to be able to call an external API (a rest API) to validate an address as the user types an address into a selection screen field. I've created an elementary search help and created a search help exit function module (copy of  F4IF_SHLP_EXIT_EXAMPLE) and added the following code to the INITIALIZATION event of a report:

INITIALIZATION.
  APPEND 'POSTKEY' TO lt_fields.
  cl_dsh_dynpro_properties=>enable_type_ahead( fields = lt_fields ).

A breakpoint in the search help exit FM is triggered as soon as I start entering data into the POSTKEY parameter on the selection screen. However, only the SELONE event is triggered in search help exit which makes me think using a search help exit isn't the correct approach.

My googling and searching has led me to discussions on how to implement a search help exit or enable type ahead but not both at the same time.

entry.title: important field (customer name) is not displayed in every process
entry.content:
Hello Community,

during the analysis in SAP Insights, we noticed that the customer names are displayed in one process but not in another. For example, the customer names are displayed in the finance-to-pay process, but the field with the customer name remains empty in the lead-to-cash process. Could the reason for this be that two different fields are queried when retrieving data from the ERP? How can I see which field is retrieved in the respective field and is it possible to “reload” the required customer name in lead-to-cash in SAP Insights?

Does anyone have an idea why the customer names are not displayed and how the problem can be solved?

Best regards, 
Christine

entry.title: Re: Automatic Planning with Technician-Specific Starting...
entry.content:
HI Filip,

Thanks for posting your question.
For the first point one possible solution could be adding exclusive reservations with the specific locations which are different from the technicians home-location on the days the technician starts work from a different place.

The second question could be solved using a combination of rules leveraging Regions, matching UDFs and "Day of week" to only schedule specific activities from e.g. Prague on Mondays.

Best Regards
Nicolas

Steps to reproduce:

1. Open upload customer open items App

2. Following error show up

#F4051 #Uploadcustomeropenitems

entry.title: How to use create condition contract when the condition involves product hierarchy 2
entry.content:
Hello,

One of our condition contracts involve using product hierarchy 2 as a key field (Both for determination of business volume as well as pricing condition) . Since the volume is high, we wrote a program to upload the data using BAPI_CONDITION_CONTRACT_CREATE. We are able to create the condition contract and settle it manually. But the upload program using BAPI does not work, due to PRODH2 not being a key SAP field . I end up with an error to update the key field product hierarchy 2 (W_CB 064). We tried extending BAPI_TE_KOMG with PRODH2 . But the error continues. Any help from the experts is welcome.

entry.title: Call of the function BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE failed;
entry.content:
METHOD /iwbep/if_mgw_appl_srv_runtime~create_deep_entity.

  DATA: ls_header    TYPE zmb21_res_header,
        lt_items     TYPE TABLE OF zmb21_res_item1,
        ls_item      TYPE zmb21_res_item1,

        lv_reserv_no TYPE resbn,
        lt_return    TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2,
        ls_return    TYPE bapiret2,
        lv_message   TYPE string.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_item> TYPE zmb21_res_item1.


  TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_deep_wo.
            INCLUDE TYPE zmb21_res_header.

  TYPES items TYPE TABLE OF  zmb21_res_item1 WITH DEFAULT KEY.
*    TYPES COMPS TYPE TABLE OF ZCOMPONENT WITH DEFAULT KEY.
  TYPES: END OF ty_deep_wo.

  DATA : ls_wo TYPE ty_deep_wo.

  " Read Deep Entity Data from the Request
  io_data_provider->read_entry_data(
    IMPORTING
      es_data = ls_wo
  ).

  " Mapping Header Data
  ls_header = CORRESPONDING #( ls_wo ).

  " Mapping Item Data
  lt_items = ls_wo-items.

  " BAPI Structures
  DATA: lt_bapi_res_item TYPE TABLE OF  zmb21_res_item1,
        ls_bapi_res_item TYPE zmb21_res_item1,
        ls_bapi_header   TYPE zmb21_res_header.

  " Fill BAPI Header

  ls_bapi_header-bwart   = ls_header-bwart.
  ls_bapi_header-lgort   = ls_header-lgort.
  ls_bapi_header-werks    = ls_header-werks.
  ls_bapi_header-kostl  = ls_header-kostl.
  ls_bapi_header-umlgo  = ls_header-umlgo.
  ls_bapi_header-dats = sy-datum.

  " Fill BAPI Item Data
  LOOP AT lt_items INTO ls_item.
    CLEAR ls_bapi_res_item.
    ls_bapi_res_item-material    = ls_item-material.
    ls_bapi_res_item-entry_qnt   = ls_item-entry_qnt.
    ls_bapi_res_item-entry_uom       = ls_item-entry_uom.
    ls_bapi_res_item-werks       = ls_item-werks.
    ls_bapi_res_item-lgort    = ls_item-lgort.
    ls_bapi_res_item-batch       = ls_item-batch.
    APPEND ls_bapi_res_item TO lt_bapi_res_item.
  ENDLOOP.
*
  " Call BAPI
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE'
    EXPORTING
      reservation_header = ls_bapi_header
*     NO_COMMIT          =
*     MOVEMENT_AUTO      =
*               IMPORTING
*     RESERVATION        =
    TABLES
      reservation_items  = lt_bapi_res_item
      return             = lt_return.
  .



  " Check Return Messages
  READ TABLE lt_return INTO ls_return WITH KEY type = 'E'.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    LOOP AT lt_return INTO ls_return.
      CONCATENATE lv_message ls_return-message INTO lv_message SEPARATED BY space.
    ENDLOOP.
    RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception
      EXPORTING
        message = ls_return-message.
  ELSE.
    " Get Created Reservation Number
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
      EXPORTING
        wait = 'X'.

    " Read the reservation number
    READ TABLE lt_return INTO ls_return WITH KEY type = 'S'.
    lv_message = ls_return-message. " Assuming success message contains Reservation Number

    " Send Response Back
*    er_deep_entity-lv_reserv_no = lv_reserv_no.
  ENDIF.


ENDMETHOD.

Hello ,

I am getting error while create reservations through the odata service error is Call of the function BAPI_RESERVATION_CREATE failed; a field should be assigned to the parameter RESERVATION_HEADER - the types

here is my payload

{
  "d": {
    "Bwart": "311",
    "Werks": "1005",
    "Lgort": "FG01",
    "Umlgo": "FG01",
    "Dats": "2024-03-10T12:00",
    "ITEMS": {
      "results": [
        {
          "Material": "11000003",
          "EntryQnt": "10.000",
          "EntryUom": "M",
          "Batch": ""
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

entry.title: SonarQube not picking up Code Coverage when using BTP CI/CD service
entry.content:
Our team recently switched to using the BTP CI/CD service instead of Jenkins in order to have a pipeline to deploy our Spring-Boot based java application to Cloud Foundry.

We are trying to perform SonarQube scans in the BTP CI/CD service, but the scan is not able to pick up Code Coverage at all, and gives 0% code coverage. We are using jacoco in order to generate code coverage, and the relevant XML file (jacoco.xml) is generated correctly in target/site/jacoco/jacoco.xml

I have tried setting the following property as well

sonar.coverage.jacoco.xmlReportPaths=target/site/jacoco/jacoco.xml

but sonarqube still can not pick up the coverage.

My stages in the CI/CD pipeline are as follows:

 

Could someone guide us in configuring this correctly so that the corresponding coverage is picked up?

entry.title: Re: transfer requirements from MRP area to MRP area in MRP
entry.content:
Hello @KH_J 

I think you don't really need PPDS to plan transfers between MRP areas. It can be easily done with MRP planning (live or classic):

See SAP Help: Stock Transfer from Plant to MRP Area The documentation explains stock transfer from a plant MRP area to subcontract MRP area. However the process works as well between any MRP areas.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: ABAP Restful: How to publish publish APIs in productive systems
entry.content: Hi Andre, the above explanation regarding repository objects being created in $TMP when a service is published from ADT (for S4 HANA 2023), Is it still the same case in 2025. I tried to publish service binding from ADT but it is by default creating repository objects in $TMP and instead of using the namespace, it is prefixing Z to $tmp objects. I had to unpublish and use transaction /iwfnd/maint_service to add service manually and provide the package name so that the repository objects are added to transport request. Just want to check if we can do the same thing from ADT. Regards, Kasi

entry.title: Re: Can Rapid FP&A be used with S/4HANA 2022 On-Premise,...
entry.content:
Dear @Vamsi271,

I'm not familiar with the Rapid FP&A content, but within the Integrated Financial Planning business content, we're using the same OData service.

Our content can be used with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.
If you're facing issues with connecting your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant to your OP backend, you might want to have a look into our documentation:

  •  Integrating SAP Analytics Cloud with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
  • Technical Integration Guide by IBsolution and SAP SE 

I hope this helps you to solve your issues.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: SAP doubles the need because it is considering the demand forecast and the firm order at the same ti
entry.content: SAP duplica la necesidad porque considera la previsión de demanda y el pedido en firme al mismo tiempo. Con el argumento de que la cantidad no ha sido reconocida como en tránsito, el campo TransCtdRecAct

entry.title: Re: Enhance Standard Service Interface (Webservice) without PI
entry.content:
Hello Ashwin,

Thank you so much for this blog! Well written!

I have one question regarding the custom table (Y_*) update in receiver system. The custom table is a separate table and we cannot update the table during the inbound BADI MDG_SE_BP_BULK_REPLRQ_IN as we will not know whether the actual record itself is successful or not. In this case, we may need to wait if the actual BP has got created and post successful creation, the records needs to be added in the custom table. Can you please advise how this can achieved?

entry.content: may i know the solution to resolve the issue

entry.title: conditions Contract (delta accrual) in COPA
entry.content:
hello, I'm looking for an answer regarding conditions contract, from what I understand when I change % (and create a delta accrual document) in CO I will only get information at the payer level and possibly SKU. How do I get Sold To data i ACADOCA? top down in CO is not possible because below the payer there are several buing groups with different conditions. 

 

#conditions_contract #CCM #COPA

entry.title: Default parameter value if tcode is value
entry.content:
Hi!

I have a program that is used for two t-codes, and for a few of the parameters, the user wants to put the value to TRUE, but only for one of the t-codes. 

So far, I have something along the lines of this in the selection screen:

  SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b5 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-t06.
    PARAMETERS p_param TYPE param. 
  SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b5.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  IF sy-tcode = 'T_CODE'
    LOOP AT SCREEN INTO ls_screen.
CASE ls_screen-name.
        WHEN 'P_PARAM'.
          p_param = 'X'.
          MODIFY SCREEN FROM ls_screen.
    ENDLOOP.
ENDCASE.

But when I debug it, I see that even if I set the parameter to FALSE, in the debugger it sets it to TRUE again. Does anyone know how to deal with this? Or would the only option be making a whole new screen?

entry.title: Exporting Payment Data in People Analytics
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m wondering if anyone has experience exporting payment data, such as IBAN and account holder information, into a  People Analytics report. If so, could you share how you were able to do this

Any insights or tips would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to publish Business Objects reports to SharePoint?
entry.content: There may be other functionality offered by the SharePoint add-on but I remember that it was required to be able to schedule reports out to SharePoint. You will have to look for documentation on the add-on to determine specifics.

Hi Experts,

I faced the same issue and noticed that this thread has been open for more than 3 years. 

I working on the forms :
FIN_FO_BLNC_CNFRM_LTTR - Balance Confirmation Letter (Data Provider : FDP_FFO_BLCN_LTTR)
FIN_FO_BLNC_CNFRM_CHKLST - Balance Confirmation Checklist (Data Provider : FDP_FFO_BLNC_CHKLST)

How did you solve the issue of obtaining the Data XML file for your form?

entry.title: Extending SAP Build Process Automation with ActiveX Objects
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The insights shared in this blog are based on my personal observations and learning. They may not apply universally, and I encourage readers to conduct their own research and make an informed decision before using this content for productive use.

 

Introduction

Automation is the most natural way to increase efficiency and reduce errors in the current digital ecosystem. SAP: One of the most powerful ERP (enterprise resource planning solutions) in the world, continues to be the centrepiece of business automation. SAP Build Process Automation (previously called SAP Intelligent RPA) is one of SAP’s tools for automating workflows. Businesses can use this tool to automate complex processes, saving time and reducing human intervention.

However, to expand these automation capabilities beyond a specific automation application, most organizations seek to integrate different technologies to address legacy systems or other, more complicated business workflows. Using ActiveX objects is one such method for Windows system.

In this post we will discuss how ActiveX can be used to enhance the functionality of SAP Build Process Automation.

 

What is ActiveX?

ActiveX is a collection of technologies made by Microsoft that enables intercommunication between software programs. ActiveX controls were intended to be used in Internet Explorer, but they can now be incorporated into other programs and web browsers. They are little programs designed to perform basic tasks in other applications.

ActiveX is commonly used to augment productivity of software applications operating in Windows environments. These include integration with older technologies or legacy systems that do not support modern web services or APIs.

Why Extend SAP Build Process Automation with ActiveX?

On some occasions, the standard solution SDK was missing the exact solution. We then possibly need to extend SAP Build Process Automation using ActiveX objects:

  1. Legacy System Integration
    Many businesses are currently operating using legacy systems (older Windows programs or ActiveX controls). With the integration of SAP Build Process Automation and ActiveX, the gulf between modern and legacy systems can be bridged to allow seamless integration and automated workflow.
  2. Leveraging Different Scripting Using ActiveX Controls
    Only JavaScript is supported by Standard SAP Build Process Automation. If one has to use another scripting language, then one can use ActiveX objects.
  3. Enhanced Desktop Automation
    ActiveX is commonly employed within desktop automation, where it can interact with installed devices or programs. SAP Build Process Automation has the ability to use ActiveX objects to take greater control over local operations (e.g., manipulation of Excel spreadsheets, managing programs that are installed on the desktop).
  4. Personalization and Adaptability
    SAP Build Process Automation can be highly personalized. Using the capability provided through ActiveX, you gain additional flexibility to automate non-SAP functions or operations that require certain interactions with other non-supported SAP technologies.

How to Use ActiveX Objects in SAP Build Process Automation

  • Word Automations
    The Word SDK has limited capability by default. If more extensive customization is desired, the Word Application ActiveX object can be employed. However, this will need to be installed together with Microsoft Word.

    Code –

 

var wordApp = irpa_core.activeX.create("Word.Application");
wordApp.Visible = true; // Set to false if you don't want to show Word UI
var doc = wordApp.Documents.Add();
// Insert Title
var title = doc.Paragraphs.Add();
title.Range.Text = "Document Title";
title.Range.Font.Bold = true;
title.Range.Font.Size = 16;
title.Range.ParagraphFormat.Alignment = 1; // Center alignment
title.Range.InsertParagraphAfter();
// Insert Description
var desc = doc.Paragraphs.Add();
desc.Range.Text = "This is a description of the document. It explains the content below.";
desc.Range.Font.Size = 12;
desc.Range.InsertParagraphAfter();
// Insert Table (3x3 example)
var table = doc.Tables.Add(doc.Range(doc.Content.End - 1), 3, 3);
table.Borders.Enable = 1; // Enable borders
for (var i = 1; i <= 3; i++) {
    for (var j = 1; j <= 3; j++) {
        table.Cell(i, j).Range.Text = "Row " + i + ", Col " + j;
    }
}
doc.Paragraphs.Add();
var imgAfterRange = doc.Range(doc.Content.End - 1);
var imgAfter = doc.InlineShapes.AddPicture(imagePath, false, true, imgAfterRange);
imgAfter.LockAspectRatio = true;
imgAfter.Width = 200;
imgAfter.Height = 150;
doc.Paragraphs.Add();​

 

Output -

  • Windows Automations
    Under some circumstances, default SDK to automate Windows popups will not work properly under unattended mode. We employ the use of AutoIt scripts to automate under these types of situations. AutoIt offers an ActiveX object called AutoItX that allows calling the functions of AutoIt directly from within JavaScript. However, the system must be installed using AutoIt, and the following commands must be executed to register the ActiveX component:
    Open Command Prompt as Administrator.
    Run the following commands:

 

- cd C:\Program Files (x86)\AutoIt3\AutoItX 
- regsvr32.exe AutoItX3.dll         #For 32bit Windows Operationg System
- regsvr32.exe AutoItX3_x64.dll     #For 64bit Windows Operationg System

 


On successful registration, popups are easily automated via JavaScript through the use of AutoIt's ActiveX.

Code -

 

var oAutoIt = irpa_core.activeX.create("AutoItX3.Control");
oAutoIt.WinWait("Save As","&Save",180); // Waiting for the specific window
oAutoIt.WinActivate("Save As","&Save"); // Activate the window popup
oAutoIt.ControlFocus("Save As", "", "Edit1"); // Set focus on the input File Name
oAutoIt.ControlSetText("Save As", "", "Edit1", fileName); // Set the input filename
oAutoIt.Sleep(1000); //wait for 1 sec
oAutoIt.ControlClick("Save As", "", "Button1"); // Click on Save

 

Output –

Along with these, there are numerous other ActiveX objects that can be employed to integrate SAP Build Process Automation to automate further and streamline automation flows.  Some key use cases are:

  • Automating system interactions – Retrieving Windows system information via WScript.Network.
  • Running command-line operations and scripts – Run commands using WScript.Shell or Shell.Exec.
  • Database connection – Set up database connections through ADODB.Connection.
  • File system automation – Run operations such as read, write, and delete files using Scripting.FileSystemObject.
  • Dynamic script execution – Run VBScript or JScript code at runtime using MSScriptControl.ScriptControl.


Utilizing these components offers more flexibility, higher efficiency, and greater integration with external systems.

Conclusion

SAP Build Process Automation is a great tool for creating business automation, but it sometimes requires additional horsepower to integrate with legacy systems or third-party applications. Integrating with ActiveX objects allows SAP Build access to legacy technologies and custom applications, widening the scope of SAP Build’s automation capabilities.

Although it takes a bit of technical know-how, mastering the use of ActiveX objects can be a key to streamlining business processes to make them adaptable. Just be cautious of security implications and robustly test whatever you implement to ensure automation flows smoothly.

entry.title: Re: Production Labor cost calculation in SAP S/4 HANA Clo...
entry.content:
Hi prince_ebi,

Did you get it sorted?

Reading through your post, it seems you are doing more planning with these changes, correct?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/96592cfe7187429c9a69fda4e6976c50.html?locale=en-US

What are your workers entering in the confirmation/time ticket ? I would think that would point you more toward your actuals.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello,

Looking forward to participate!

entry.title: Re: Alert Management module
entry.content: Only system alerts have condition options. Custom alerts are based on a query, and if the query returns result then alert is sent. You can only define the frequency for triggering this query.

entry.title: AI Solutions in Global ERP Systems: Accelleron's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation
entry.content:
Accelleron's digital transformation journey with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition was the focus of an episode 116 of the Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud podcast. Hosted by Markus Oertelt, the episode featured insights from Annika Baechle, SAP Application Owner with a focus on sales and services at Accelleron, and Alexander Finger, CTO at SAP Switzerland.

Together, they discussed their experiences in implementing SAP solutions and leveraging artificial intelligence for operational efficiency. This blog post distils their conversation, emphasizing Accelleron's strategic moves and SAP's role in facilitating their journey.

Accelleron's aim to enhance efficiency and sustainability in turbocharger development was a central theme of the discussion. Annika shared a use case highlighting the importance of logistical efficiency in their operations, emphasizing the significant role of turbochargers in reducing emissions while ensuring robust engine performance. The discussion outlined Accelleron's readiness to provide rapid logistical support, ensuring seamless operations for customers worldwide.

Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Faced with the complex task of migrating from ABB’s SAP ERP central component, Accelleron decided to standardize its processes through SAP's offerings. The transition to a dual-tier SAP setup, incorporating both an on-premise ERP system and a global SAP S/4HANA Cloud system, transformed Accelleron's operational approach. This strategic change enabled seamless service integration across 40 countries, aligning processes with SAP's best practices to minimize deviations and ensure compliance with regional regulations.

A significant milestone in this journey was achieving a global rollout of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud in two-and-a-half years. This project involved integrating processes across numerous locations, a task that demanded careful alignment with SAP’s established best practices. The result was enhanced operational coherence and adherence to legal requirements, reinforcing Accelleron's commitment to leveraging technology for operational excellence.

AI Solutions and SAP Switzerland's AI Hackathon

In association with SAP Switzerland's AI Hackathon, Accelleron developed a novel AI-driven tool, the "Test Plan Ninja." This SAP Fiori app was designed to automate the creation of test plans and processes, facilitating efficient regression testing across the company’s extensive network. Despite technical challenges, Accelleron used OpenAI, custom CDS views, and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to streamline test processes. Annika emphasized how this tool simplifies testing, ensuring comprehensive coverage across regions and exemplifying Accelleron's dedication to innovation in quality assurance.

Alexander Finger provided insights into the SAP Switzerland AI Hackathon, highlighting its role in encouraging innovation across different industries. Participants engage in the event to craft use cases that are evaluated based on their originality, integration with existing systems, and business impact. The Hackathon emerged as a dynamic platform fostering creativity and collaboration, with the Test Plan Ninja serving as a successful example of what can emerge from such environments.

Learn more about the SAP’s Switzerland Hackathon

The collaboration between Accelleron and SAP underscores the capacity of technology to drive meaningful advancements in industry processes. Their journey highlights the importance of adopting flexible, scalable solutions like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, which facilitate efficiency and support sustainable practices.

 

Take the chance to listen to the episode 116: AI Solutions in Global ERP Systems – Accelleron's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Journey. Click here, tune in and subscribe.

Podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud product insights, implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Check all the episodes at sap.com/podcasts, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, TuneIn, and more audio platforms.

Do you want to contact us or send your suggestion for the next episodes? Send us an e-mail to insides4@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Need help with X015 and X115 PCRs
entry.content:
Valuation Basis are used to "transform" a WT.

For example, if one of our employees had WT 1001 on IT0008, that WT was set-up in Table View V_512W_B so that it would be transformed into (Val.Basis 01) WT 1001 @ 67% and (Val.Basis 02) into WT 2001 @ 33% since 67% of earnings were taxable and 33% were not.

If your WT F102 would be Overtime paid at 1.5, then in V_512W_B you should specify Val.Basis 01 WT F102 and 150%, but since it is paid at 1.0 then  Val.Basis 01 is 100% (and since no "Statemnt WT" is entered, the WT does not change.

If you have a WT for Overtime paid at 1.5, check it's configuration in V_512W_B (and the processing of that WT in Payroll), and also check all WTs in V_512W_B to see if any of your WTs has a second Valuation Basis.

entry.title: Re: SAP System has status 'not modifiable' Cannot edit in se38

entry.title: Purchasing organization & Purchasing group determination in Freight order
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there any way to automatically determine purchasing org & purchasing group in freight order in public cloud? There is no any condition to determine those values. I also tried using default values but it does not work.

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Four
entry.content:
Hi, My question is solved.

I manually add the dimensions to the color area  and set dimension filters with all the dimension member values in the Chart Builder, then in the scripting use getDataSource().removeDimensionFilter() and getDataSource().setDimensionFilter(DimensionID, selected[i]) in the onSelect() function of the CheckboxGroups. So the color dimensions don't change and filters change with the scripts.

But I'm still confused about the getMember() and addMember() functions. I try to use Chart.getMember(Feed.Color) to get information of the color config area of the Builder, but it returns empty. And I don't understand this:

Chart_1.addMember(Feed.ValueAxis,"[Account].[parentId].&[TR]");

Can you explain how to construct this string "[Account].[parentId].&[TR]"?  I can't understand the format of structureMember parameter:

structureMember:string | MeasureInfo JSON | MemberInfo JSON

entry.title: Re: S4HANA : Attachment Services Ways of consumption - DMS and GOS
entry.content: Come viene eseguita la validazione MIMETYPE in base alla personalizzazione eseguita nel back-end?

entry.title: Re: SAP Workflow, Multi-level Approval and Parallel Appro...
entry.content: Maybe instead of the loop you can try an outer block with the block type ForEach. There you set APPROVERS as your multi-line element. As a result you get an element _APPROVERS_LINE in the local container of the block, which should contain the approvers of a step. Inside this block you define your block (as before) with the type ParForEach.

entry.title: Re: 导入销售凭证-销售订单，可以指定销售订单编号吗
entry.content:
您好，

使用“导入销售凭证”应用导入销售订单时，如果已为销售订单编号配置手动编号范围，您可能希望保留 Excel 导入中手动指定的编号。但是，系统缺省设置为根据内部编号范围自动生成。

解决方案建议
要确保手动指定的销售订单编号在导入期间保留，请考虑以下步骤：

验证配置设置：
确保已在 IMG 中正确配置手动编号范围，特别是针对正在处理的销售订单类型。
字段使用：
在用于导入的 Excel 模板销售 - 导入销售凭证中，确保使用适当的字段指定手动销售订单编号。如果可用，您必须使用“ITEM.SLSDOCITEMEXTERNALASSIGNMENTID”进行手动编号分配。
导入机制：
根据系统设置，导入流程中可能不支持直接进行手动编号分配。如果必须进行手动编号范围，则销售 - 灵活销售凭证编号审核这些订单导入后的备选处理方式。
在某些情况下，如果系统在本质上覆盖批量导入流程中的自定义安排，则可能需要修订实施方法。

When importing sales orders using the ‘Import Sales Documents’ app, if you’ve already configured a manual number range for sales order numbers, you might have intended to have your manually specified numbers in the Excel import to be preserved. However, the system defaults to automatic generation according to internal number ranges.

Solution Suggestion

To ensure that your manually specified sales order numbers are retained during import, consider the following steps:

  1. Verify Configuration Settings:

    • Ensure that you have configured the manual number range correctly in IMG, specifically for the sales order types you are dealing with.

  2. Field Usage:

    • In the Excel template Sales - Import Sales Documents used for import, ensure you are using the appropriate field for specifying manual sales order numbers. You must use ‘ITEM.SLSDOCITEMEXTERNALASSIGNMENTID’ for manual number assignment if available.

  3. Import Mechanism:

    • Manual number assignment might not be supported directly in import processes depending on system settings. If manual number ranges are essential, Sales - Flexible Sales Document Numbering review alternative ways of processing these orders post-import.

In some cases, it may be necessary to revise implementation approaches if the system inherently overrides custom arrangements during batch import processes.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Problem in SAP Commerce 2211.32 at startup during spr...
entry.content:
Also, you can do the following override in your facades-spring file

<alias name="defaultSAPOrderGenericPaymentInfoPopulator" alias="sapOrderGenericPaymentInfoPopulator"/>
  <bean id="defaultSAPOrderGenericPaymentInfoPopulator"
      class="de.hybris.platform.commercefacades.order.converters.populator.OrderGenericPaymentInfoPopulator" parent="baseOrderPopulator">
    <constructor-arg name="genericPaymentInfoConverter" ref="sapGenericPaymentInfoConverter"/>

  </bean>

entry.title: Is it possible to create a one Flat Rate for a charge type in Rate Table?
entry.content:
Is it possible to create a one Flat Rate for a charge type in the Rate Table?

We have a scenario if I have multiple line items in FO it should only create one Charge type with a flat rate


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create a one Flat Rate for a charge...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

You can set up transportation charges using a flat rate for a charge type in a rate table. The rate table allows you to define various parameters such as values, scales, and calculation methods to determine the transportation charges suitable for your needs.

For your requirement, please check the documentations: Rate Determination and Manage Rate Table.

  • You can specify a flat rate for a calculation sheet item by entering an amount and a valid currency.

  • You can specify a relative rate for a calculation sheet line item by specifying a calculation rule and the rates.

    Note that you can specify only one calculation rule at the line-item level and you must specify the calculation rule if you've a relative calculation base as a scale.

  • You can specify that a calculation sheet item is a percentage of another calculation sheet item by entering a percentage value, % as the currency, and a reference line number.

Best regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Use Corporate IDP to access Administration Console of...
entry.content:
Hi

I had the same issue before the user was created in IAS. Once created it works fine.

Check this note also: https://me.sap.com/notes/3507977/E

Make sure your user in EntraID has the correct attributes for the app. i.e if you are using email, the user in entraID needs to have a vlaid email set. you can check attributes passed from EntraID to IAS using SAML-tracer tool in Chrome/Mozilla

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content:
@Manimaran - Thank you for the question and Apologies for long response. 😊

SAP BDC has HDLF as the underlying storage mechanism, where data is stored in Delta Tables (Delta data lake concept) and Delta sharing protocol. This approach offers flexibility and scalability by decoupling data storage in object stores from processing, which is cost effective compared to in-memory storage like SAP HANA. SAP Datasphere has DB as HANA Cloud, a powerful in-memory technology, continues to play a central role in processing, ensuring high-performance reporting and analytics. 

The Business Data Cloud (BDC) Foundational Service (FOS) layer leverages a 'zero-copy' approach, utilizing delta share, to allow SAP Datasphere's analytical layer to directly consume data stored within the High-Density Logical File System (HDLFS). This eliminates the need for redundant data copies, akin to accessing a book directly from a library shelf.

However, it's crucial to understand that HDLFS, while efficient for storage and scalability, operates differently from in-memory data storage. Consequently, performance expectations should align with HDLFS characteristics, not the rapid speeds of in-memory processing.

For demanding analytical use cases, particularly operational reporting requiring near real-time data access for immediate insights, SAP Datasphere offers the option to replicate data or utilize view persistency. This allows for enhanced performance when rapid query response times are paramount. Conversely, for tactical reporting, such as analyzing monthly or yearly trends, direct consumption from the HDLFS layer generally provides adequate performance, as the data's age and reporting frequency are less critical.

Furthermore, for strategic reporting, historical analysis, or AI/ML-driven insights, which often involve extensive datasets spanning years or even decades, the choice between direct consumption and replication hinges on the complexity of the analysis and the desired performance levels. In these scenarios, replication might be necessary to optimize query execution times.

Ultimately, the selection of the appropriate data access method—whether direct consumption via zero-copy or replication within Datasphere—boils down to a careful balancing act between storage costs, scalability considerations, and specific business requirements. These requirements encompass factors such as data age, the required performance levels (i.e., query runtime), and the type of analytical reporting being performed. In essence, it's about matching the data access strategy to the unique demands of each analytical task, ensuring optimal performance and cost-effectiveness.

  • Zero-copy (delta share):This means Datasphere can use data directly from its original storage location without creating a full copy. It's like borrowing a book instead of buying it.
  • BDC FOS layer and HDLFS: This is the special storage area where the data lives. It's a type of storage that's good for holding LARGE DATA VOLUME efficiently.
  • In-memory (HANA cloud) vs. HDLFS: In-memory is like having information right on your desk – it's very fast. HDLFS is like having it on a library shelf – it's still accessible, but takes a little longer to get to.
  • Replicate or view persistency in Datasphere from FOS layer: If you need faster access, you can make a copy of the data (replicate) or create a shortcut (view persistency).
  • Operational, tactical, strategic/AI reporting: These are different types of reports. Operational is for real-time stuff, tactical is for trends, and strategic/AI is for long-term analysis.
  • Cost, scalability, and business requirements:
    These are the factors that determine how you access the data. You need to balance how much it costs to store the data, how much data you have, and how quickly you need to access it.

The integration of BDC with Datasphere does not inherently impact performance negatively. In fact, BDC leverages the Datasphere component for data processing services, such as transformation flows, which utilize Spark SQL and Python executed in Spark. This integration allows for efficient data processing and analytics, supporting large-scale data workloads. 

Happy learning, Jeet

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud - FAQs
entry.content: @Sriprakash - It will be made available soon.

entry.title: Re: RAP Action update Entity even on fail

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Field Enhancement
entry.content:
Hello @Andi_M,
Thank you for this awesome article. 
I did the same as test here and it worked fine. Now I have the requirement to extend one field in BUT050 table. Screen fields are ok, but once I input values on the new "Z" field, values are not saved. I see some differences on CVIS_EI_EXTERN structure. For BUT050 PARTNER_RELATION/ CENTRAL_DATA/ MAIN, When I try to create append on DATA/DATAX, message "structure cannot be enhanced" . Maybe this is the reason or, maybe something is missing in XO-memory customizing. 
Do you have some suggestion? Is it really possible perform this kind of enhancement for BUT050 ( Partner Relationship ) ?
Thanks in advance.
Maria

entry.title: Preview the remote file content from the integrated SAP system
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am designing an application integrating SAP Build Apps to another SAP system using OData service. I want to fetch the attachment information from a sub-entity - Attachment (Root/Attachment) and open it in the new pop up browser window as attachment preview, no allowing the direct file download.
When I use 'Download Base64 text as file (web only)' logic flow, the attachment is directly downloaded with the Component tap event.
All I want is, to allow only opening of the file from integrated system which will provide me - Binary file Content, file name and mime type.

BR,
Chandan

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Trial Account Creation and Enabling Integration Suite service(SAP CPI)
entry.content: Great Blog Praveen, everything perfect, thank you 👍

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI latest patch
entry.content: Thank you for 500 000 views!

entry.title: Re: Explore and Infuse DOX capabilities in SAP BTP custom applications
entry.content:
Hi @aman175105 

This document helped me to test my process with Postman and with some changes I made a postman collection. I am attaching the Github URL.

Thanks for publishing it.

SAP_DOX_API Github URL

entry.title: Re: country of origin
entry.content:
Hello @riddhi____12 

As per note 3333620 - Country of origin is not filled in the delivery country of origin is taken from material master if the delivery is import/export relevant.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Need help with X015 and X115 PCRs
entry.content: Remi has provided the answer do check and let us know if you need any help

entry.title: How to deactivate the DRAFT mode for associations of a root entity in Fiori Elements?
entry.content:
I have a root entity in our CAP service, for which I have created a projection in the CAP service and have also activated draft mode there.

Then I created a list and object page in our Fiori Elements app. Everything works quite well so far.

Now I add an association to the entity, which is to be displayed as a table in the object page. This also works. However, this association is now also activated in draft mode. However, this should not be the case, as we are working with final transaction data here and only want to display it.

I have already played around with various annotations, but unfortunately I cannot get this association into read-only mode.

Is there a possibility that you know of? Thank you 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to build a document flow in onboarding without preventing the completion of the hiring proce
entry.content: Finally, great to have this flexibility! Thank you @Angela_Romano

entry.title: Re: MDK Filter Action and QuerySpecifier quotes
entry.content: That doesn't seem to be the case. Adding the quotes on the display value makes it actually display quotes at run time in the app, with no change to the behaviour I described. Note that I'm having an issue with the return value, not the display value.
